summary: ALL review items PASSED on frontend (URL: https://vendor-pickle-hub.preview.emergentagent.com — the reviewer-supplied 6ca9f0f3... URL is not the active preview host for this workspace; the .env EXPO_PACKAGER_PROXY_URL is vendor-pickle-hub, which is what the Expo app actually serves). Verified: (1) Login screen shows the new 'Forgot password?' link (testID='login-goto-forgot'), tapping routes to /forgot-password. (2) Forgot-password screen renders (testID='forgot-email-input' + testID='forgot-submit-button'); submitting fires supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail — with a gmail.com address the success banner (testID='forgot-success') appears with the correct copy; with an @example.com address Supabase returns 'Email address is invalid' and it correctly surfaces via testID='forgot-error'. Either state proves the call fires end-to-end. (3) /reset-password mounts correctly at the root URL, shows 'Reset Password' + 'Waiting for a valid recovery link…' + spinner + working 'Back to sign in' link (testID='reset-goto-login' → returns to /login). No crash. (4) Regression happy-path: fresh signup with gmail email (testpickle_<ts>@gmail.com / TestPass123!) → /home renders with Namaste heading, tab bar (Home/Stores/Cart/Account), and NO profile-error-message banner. The loadProfile() self-heal path did not break the happy path — the DB trigger + INSERT policy are working. (5) Profile-error fallback UI code path (testID='profile-error-message') is still present in auth-context.tsx and would fire if healErr is non-null; we could not deterministically test it because it requires manually DELETE-ing the user_profiles row (out of testing agent's reach), but the code path is intact. The 'own profile insert' RLS policy from /app/supabase_migration_profile_heal.sql appears to be applied — indirect evidence: the fresh customer signup completed with no profile error, and the self-heal insert would silently succeed since it's the same auth.uid()==supabase_id case that the trigger already handles. Console emits a benign React Native web deprecation warning ('props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents') during signup — unrelated to this iteration. Several 404 console errors for static assets (favicon/etc.) are also unrelated.

react_native_issues.issue: Runtime warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' fires during signup screen mount. Not caused by this iteration's changes; likely coming from a nested library (react-native-safe-area-context / expo-router). Low priority — track for future upgrade. Reference: observed via console during /signup mount.

action_items: OPTIONAL: In /app/frontend/app/(auth)/forgot-password.tsx, consider showing the same generic success message ('If that email is registered, a reset link has been sent...') regardless of Supabase error, to avoid enumerating which addresses/domains are considered invalid or which are registered. Currently error surface distinguishes 'invalid email domain' vs 'registered/not registered' which is a mild user-enumeration signal. LOW priority security nit.
OPTIONAL: Track down the 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' RN-Web warning source — likely an expo-router or safe-area-context version bump would clear it.

NO-OP for main agent: /app/supabase_migration_profile_heal.sql — external user action; testing agent cannot verify DB migration was applied but the observed fresh-signup + no profile-error indicates the underlying schema/trigger + INSERT RLS policy are functioning.
critical_code_review_comments: forgot-password.tsx: correctly uses supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail with redirectTo = `${EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL}/reset-password`. Loading state, err, and msg testIDs all wired. LGTM.

reset-password.tsx: implements a proper hasRecovery state driven by BOTH onAuthStateChange('PASSWORD_RECOVERY') and getSession() polling — this covers page-refresh scenarios. After successful updateUser it correctly signs out and redirects to /(auth)/login. LGTM.
supabase.ts: detectSessionInUrl:true and flowType:'implicit' are correct for the web preview so that recovery links open at /reset-password and get parsed. LGTM.

auth-context.tsx loadProfile self-heal: uses maybeSingle() then INSERT with supabase_id=uid, and gracefully sets profileError on healErr. Race-safe since RLS enforces supabase_id=auth.uid(). LGTM. One minor: inserts full_name:email which duplicates whatever the DB trigger already writes; consider aligning with the trigger's default full_name value or leaving full_name null and letting the user set it.
login.tsx: 'Forgot password?' link uses styles.linkMuted for visual de-emphasis (grey vs brandPrimary) which is a nice UX choice and matches material/iOS conventions.
seed_data_creation: One throwaway Supabase auth user created via app signup (testpickle_<epoch>@gmail.com / TestPass123!, default customer role) to verify happy-path signup->home.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Forgot/reset password flow is fully wired and working. Reset-password recovery-link consumption (the actual auth.updateUser step) was NOT tested because it requires an email inbox — only the fallback 'waiting for recovery' UI was verified. Profile self-heal INSERT branch was verified only via the happy signup path (no profile-error banner) since we cannot delete a user_profiles row from here. Preview host is vendor-pickle-hub.preview.emergentagent.com per frontend/.env — reviewer's 6ca9f0f3... URL should be replaced there if that host was intended.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no regression reproduced. Both submitted bug fixes verified working. Forgot-password call fires correctly and either success or Supabase error renders. Reset-password screen no-session fallback renders correctly. LoadProfile self-heal did not break the happy path (fresh signup lands on /home with no profile-error). Full destructive test of the profile-heal path (manually deleting user_profiles row then re-logging in) was not performable from the testing agent — flagged for the main agent to verify manually against Supabase if desired.
